message:
Migrate entire HTTP/WebSocket/channel stack from tokio+reqwest to asupersync

This is the culmination of the async runtime migration: every HTTP
client, WebSocket connection, channel primitive, and async select/spawn
call now routes through asupersync instead of tokio+reqwest+tungstenite.

fcp-async-core: Rewrote the channel module from scratch on top of
asupersync channels. The mpsc, broadcast, and oneshot implementations
now use lock-free VecDeque+AtomicBool+Waker internally instead of
tokio::sync primitives. The select! macro now delegates to
futures_util::select!/select_biased! with automatic .fuse() wrapping
instead of tokio::select!. Removed task_local! macro (replaced by
explicit context stacks in prior commit). Added compatibility_cx()
helper for obtaining an asupersync Cx in non-async contexts. Total
expansion: ~1100 lines of new channel/broadcast/oneshot code.

fcp-graphql: Replaced reqwest::Client with asupersync::http::h1::
HttpClient. Headers now stored as Vec<(String,String)> instead of
reqwest::HeaderMap. Added upsert_header/header_value helpers for
case-insensitive header management. Retry-After parsing now reads from
the HeaderList directly. SchemaValidation and dedup remain unchanged.

fcp-oauth: Both OAuth1 and OAuth2 clients migrated from reqwest to
asupersync HTTP. OAuth2Client now holds Arc<HttpClient> for Clone
support. Token exchange, refresh, and revocation all use the new
HTTP client. PKCE and signature generation unchanged.

fcp-streaming: WebSocket module completely rewritten (~970 lines) to
use asupersync::net::websocket instead of tokio-tungstenite. New
helpers: websocket_cx(), websocket_config(), socket_addr(),
build_handshake(). TLS connections use asupersync::tls::TlsStream.
SSE module updated for asupersync HTTP responses. Error types updated
to map asupersync WsError variants.

fcp-tailscale: Tailscale client migrated from reqwest to asupersync
HTTP. Error type updated to wrap HttpClientError.

fcp-host: Host binary and doctor module updated for new HTTP client
and error types.

Connectors — Discord: DiscordApiClient now wraps asupersync HttpClient
instead of reqwest::Client. All REST methods (get/post/patch/put/delete)
take Method enum + method name string. Error module gains
DiscordHttpErrorInfo struct mapping all HttpClientError variants
(DNS, TLS, connect, proxy, tunnel, I/O) to structured fields.
Gateway WebSocket migrated to fcp-streaming's new WebSocket types.

Connectors — Slack: SlackClient migrated from reqwest to asupersync
HTTP. Retry logic now uses fcp_async_core::time::sleep. Error type
updated. Debug impl made manual (HttpClient doesn't derive Debug).

Connectors — GitHub, OpenAI, Bitbucket, Box, Todoist, YouTube:
HTTP clients all migrated from reqwest to asupersync. Minor API
adjustments for the new response types.

Workspace: Removed tokio and tokio-stream from workspace dependencies.
Removed tokio dev-dependency from anthropic, github, gitlab, google-ai,
openai, plaid, qdrant, telegram connectors. Updated fcp-streaming,
fcp-graphql, fcp-oauth, fcp-tailscale, fcp-host, fcp-testkit Cargo.toml
dependency lists.

Also includes minor fixes: fcp-cbor test assertion cleanup, fcp-raptorq
decode safety check, fcp-sdk ratelimit/runtime compatibility adjustments,
fcp-cli template update, fcp-e2e subprocess timeout handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
